title: WOS API Audit
description: Public API resource schema for the WOS cross-case audit query surface. Distinct from `provenance.schema.json` (per-case live record stream): audit is the cross-case retrospective query for compliance investigations, statistical evidence reviews, and identity-attestation searches. Items are projected `ProvenanceRecord` instances cross-`$ref`ed from `provenance.schema.json` (the per-case shape is the audit shape — there is no second meaning of `record`). Long-running audit queries support deferred execution: submit a query, poll its status, paginate the result. Per ADR 0082 D-15 step 6, this domain pairs with `bundle.schema.json` to close the export/cross-case surface.

AuditQuerySort.description: Closed sort taxonomy for cross-case audit results. `timestamp-asc` = oldest record first (chronological investigations); `timestamp-desc` = newest first (recent-activity reviews). Closed enum with no extension seam — sort orders are normative and a verifier MUST be able to reproduce a result set deterministically.

AuditTierFilter.description: Closed provenance-tier filter. The four tiers correspond to Kernel S8 (Facts), Governance S6.2 (Reasoning), Governance S6.4 (Counterfactual), and AI Integration S13 (Narrative). Filter aligns with the `tier` discriminator on `provenance.schema.json#/$defs/ProvenanceRecord`.

AuditQueryStatus.description: Closed lifecycle status for a deferred audit query. `pending` = queued, not yet started; `running` = executing; `succeeded` = first page available; `failed` = terminated with error; `cancelled` = caller cancelled before completion.

AuditScopeFilter.description: Closed scope filter for cross-case audit queries. Filters the query to a Tenant / Organization / Workspace / Environment slice per VISION §V scope hierarchy. The shape gates on ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed) — when ADR 0068 promotes to Accepted, this object becomes the wire form audit scope is reported in. Until then, server implementations MAY accept only the fields they have settled. Cross-tenant audit is structurally inexpressible by construction: a tenant filter is REQUIRED, and the runtime refuses cross-tenant reads (ADR 0068 D-1).

tenant.description: Tenant identifier filter — outermost hosting/commercial boundary (ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). REQUIRED. The runtime refuses queries whose `tenant` does not match the caller's authenticated tenant scope.

organization.description: Optional organization identifier filter — product-ownership boundary (ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Restricts the result set to one organization within the tenant.

workspace.description: Optional workspace identifier filter — sub-scope inside an organization (VISION §V).

environment.description: Closed deployment environment filter. New environments require a major schema bump (no extension seam).

AuditQueryRequest.description: Cross-case audit query request body for `POST /api/v1/audit/queries`. Submits a parameterised query against the cross-case provenance corpus and (when `materialize: true`) requests durable result storage so a long-running query can be polled and paginated. The query envelope is closed: every filter is named and scoped; there is no open `criteria: Record<string, unknown>` bag.

scope.description: Tenant / Organization / Workspace / Environment scope (ADR 0068 D-2 Proposed). Tenant is REQUIRED; cross-tenant queries are refused at the runtime.

timeRange.description: Optional UTC RFC 3339 time-range filter against the provenance record `timestamp` field. Omitted to span the full retention window.

start.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 lower bound for the audit window.

end.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 upper bound for the audit window.

actorRef.description: Optional actor URN filter — restrict the query to records whose `actorRef` equals this principal (ADR 0082 D-9). Identity-attestation investigations use this filter against a specific subject.

tierFilter.description: Optional tier filter — restrict the result set to one or more provenance tiers. Empty array is forbidden; omit the field to span all four tiers.

recordKindFilter.description: Optional record-kind filter — restrict the result set to one or more Facts-tier `recordKind` literals (e.g., `identityAttestation`, `signatureAffirmation`, `caseStateMutation`). Cross-`$ref`s the closed-with-vendor-extension `FactsRecordKind` taxonomy from `provenance.schema.json`.

instanceScope.description: Optional instance scope — restrict the result set to records belonging to one or more named workflow processes. Empty array is forbidden. When omitted the query spans every visible instance in scope.

sort.description: Result-set ordering. Defaults to `timestamp-desc` when omitted.

maxResults.description: Hard upper bound on the result set, applied after every other filter. Servers MAY enforce a smaller per-deployment ceiling. The page size is governed by `limit` on the result endpoints; this bound caps the total result corpus the query materializes.

materialize.description: When true, the server materializes the result set so it can be paginated across multiple GET requests. When false (default), the query executes synchronously and `POST /api/v1/audit/queries` returns the first page directly. `Idempotency-Key` is REQUIRED when `materialize: true` (ADR 0082 D-16) because materialization is an externally-visible side effect.

AuditQueryResult.description: Cross-case audit query result envelope. Cursor-paginated per ADR 0082 D-7. `items` are projected `ProvenanceRecord` instances cross-`$ref`ed from `provenance.schema.json` — the per-case shape is the audit shape; there is no second meaning of `record`.

queryId.description: Server-issued opaque identifier for the materialized query, used to fetch subsequent pages and to cancel. Empty / synchronous queries (when `materialize: false`) MAY return a stable identifier the client can ignore.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the underlying materialized query. Synchronous queries (when `materialize: false`) return `succeeded` directly.

items.description: Projected provenance records in this page. Each item is a full tier-discriminated `ProvenanceRecord` from `provenance.schema.json` — no separate `audit-record` type is invented (audit is cross-case provenance, not a different shape).

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

submittedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the query was accepted by the server. Useful for reproducibility — a query submitted at instant T against a tenant with active appends MAY return different rows than one submitted at T+1.

completedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the query reached its terminal status. Omitted while `status in {pending, running}`.

failure.description: Failure detail when `status == failed`. Carries a stable WOS error code plus human-readable text drawn from the public registry (`error-registry.md`).

wosErrorCode.description: Stable WOS error code from the public error registry that classifies this failure.

AuditAttestationView.description: Projected identity-attestation chain view for a single subject across cases. Aggregates `IdentityAttestation` Facts-tier records (Kernel S8 / ADR 0068 D-3.1) so an investigator can answer 'what attestations did this subject hold, by which provider, with what assurance, when?' without enumerating cases by hand. Gates on PLN-0381 (stack identity-attestation ADR) — the projection shape is authored against the proposed `IdentityAttestation` record from ADR 0068 D-3.1; the wire form ratifies when PLN-0381 lands. Until then, servers MAY return only the fields they have settled and clients tolerate field absence.

subjectGlobalId.description: Tenant-independent subject identifier (URI; e.g., `did:web:...`, `urn:idp:subject:...`) per ADR 0068 D-3.1. The view aggregates every `IdentityAttestation` record naming this subject across visible cases.

attestations.description: The chain of identity-attestation records observed for this subject within the queried scope, ordered by `attestedAt` ascending. Each entry is a Facts-tier `ProvenanceRecord` whose `recordKind == identityAttestation` (cross-`$ref`ed; the audit projection is the per-case shape).

highestAssuranceLevel.description: Highest assurance level observed across the attestation chain (closed taxonomy from ADR 0068 D-3.1 + vendor-prefix extension per ADR 0082 D-12). Mirrors `wos-workflow.schema.json` `IdentityAttestationRecord.assuranceLevel`. Computed by the server from the chain; useful for compliance investigators answering 'did this subject ever reach high assurance under this provider?'.

x-wos.note: The wire vocabulary (low/standard/high/very-high) is shared with wos-workflow.schema.json identity-attestation shapes. The assurance prose (assurance.md §2.1) uses L1-L4. This mapping is the canonical cross-reference. A future taxonomy unification (ADR TBD) will align them.

earliestAttestedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the earliest attestation in the chain. Omitted when the chain is empty.

latestAttestedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the most-recent attestation in the chain. Omitted when the chain is empty.
